behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary: classifier request-construction + API client wiring. A pure Unit test of a helper can miss serialization/adapter omissions. Integration is sufficient with an in-process stub client that captures outbound payload. Rejected System because behavior text is about request content, not real network/OS effects; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-output quality or multi-step agent chain is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with a stubbed API transport, call classify(document), and assert the captured outbound request contains every configured taxonomy label (no missing/renamed labels) in the field actually sent by the client adapter.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The real failure appears when API response parsing/normalization and taxonomy validation interact. A Unit test of only the validator can miss adapter mapping bugs. Integration with a stub API response is enough. Rejected System because no real HTTP behavior is needed; rejected Agentic because correctness here does not depend on real LLM quality, only deterministic handling of an out-of-taxonomy value.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process stub API that returns a successful response containing an invalid label (e.g., "medical"). Invoke classify and assert caller receives exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Behavior directly depends on real model nondeterminism (or lack of it) across repeated invocations; deterministic stubs will always pass and can hide drift/variance failures. This is not a multi-step agent pipeline, so Workflow is not required. Rejected Integration/System alone because they do not exercise true model output variability.
behaviors.test_description: Against the real AI classification endpoint, submit the same document repeatedly (e.g., N runs under the same config) and assert label consistency criteria; fail if labels vary beyond the allowed threshold.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: To catch real budget overruns, the test should observe real provider token accounting on an actual API call; local estimators/mocks can systematically differ and hide failures. Correctness does not depend on semantic quality of generated output, so Agentic is not strictly required. Rejected Integration because in-process fakes cannot guarantee parity with real usage metering.
behaviors.test_description: Run classification on representative documents against the real API and assert reported/observed token usage per call is <= configured budget; include a guard that fails if any call exceeds budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry-policy orchestration across modules (API client error mapping + backoff/wait + retry loop). A deterministic stub that returns rate-limit then success can reproduce the failure. Rejected System because real network timing/OS primitives are not necessary to verify control flow; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-output quality or multi-agent chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API that returns a rate-limit error on first call and success on second; assert classifier does not surface the first error, performs a wait/backoff call, retries, and returns the successful label.
